Title: Incorporation of nitrogen in antinutritional Solanum alkaloid biosynthesis

Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Leaves/flower buds in tomato; leaves and fruits depending species
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Supports SGA pathway reconstitution as an upstream pathway component required for dehydrotomatidine formation in heterologous assays.
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. GAME15 was coexpressed with GAME6, GAME8, GAME11, GAME4 and candidate GABA-T homologs in N. benthamiana to reconstitute tomato SGA biosynthesis. 2. It functioned as a necessary upstream component in the assay system used to reveal GAME12/GABA-T4 activity. 3. The paper did not focus mechanistically on GAME15 beyond its use in pathway reconstitution.
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Transient Agrobacterium-mediated expression in N. benthamiana leaves
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Transient pathway reconstitution with metabolite profiling by UHPLC-MS
Common_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: GAME6; GAME8; GAME11; GAME4; GAME12/GABA-T2; GABA-T4
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Limited direct value in this paper; potentially useful as part of multigene SGA pathway engineering stacks.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Included because it was experimentally used in the Results as part of the minimal pathway reconstruction, but it is not the main mechanistic focus compared with GAME4/GAME12.

Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: γ-aminobutyric acid transaminase (GABA-T) family; PLP-dependent aminotransferase
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Nitrogen-incorporation branch point from steroidal saponins to steroidal alkaloid aglycones
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: directly forms nitrogen-containing aglycone intermediate that commits flux toward SGAs
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Substrate: 26-furostanol aldehyde; GABA (preferred amino donor); L-alanine (alternative donor, lower activity)
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Product: 26-aminofurostanol, which spontaneously cyclizes to steroidal alkaloid aglycones such as dehydrotomatidine/solasodine
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Transfers an amino group to 26-furostanol aldehyde to form 26-aminofurostanol, introducing nitrogen into the steroid backbone and enabling spontaneous F-ring closure to steroidal alkaloid aglycones.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Tomato leaves and flower buds; S. nigrum green fruits naturally, engineered leaves upon expression
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Plant defense; antinutritional toxicity and bitterness
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes de novo production of nitrogen-containing steroidal alkaloid aglycones and SGAs; controls the metabolic switch from non-nitrogenous steroidal saponins to SGAs.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. GAME12 is identical to tomato cytosolic GABA-T2 and is a neofunctionalized GABA transaminase specialized for SGA biosynthesis. 2. In N. benthamiana pathway reconstitution, GAME12 enabled formation of dehydrotomatidine. 3. In coupled in vitro assays with GAME4, GAME12 converted furostanol-derived substrate to 26-aminofurostanol and solasodine, proving the long-proposed nitrogen-incorporation step. 4. GAME12 also acted on saturated substrate to generate soladulcidine-related products. 5. Overexpression of tomato GAME12 in S. nigrum leaves was sufficient for de novo SGA production, and coexpression with GAME4 further increased SGAs and reduced uttroside B. 6. Cytosolic localization was required in planta; retargeting GAME12 to mitochondria abolished SGA formation.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Primarily expressed in leaf and flower buds
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: In S. nigrum, endogenous GAME12 ortholog detected in green fruits but not young leaves; transgenic overexpression in leaves induced SGAs
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Transient Agrobacterium expression in N. benthamiana; in vitro assays with GABA or alanine donor; stable constitutive expression in S. nigrum leaves
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Transient pathway reconstitution; in vitro coupled enzyme assay with GAME4 and Rapidase; metabolite profiling by UHPLC-MS; stable overexpression in S. nigrum; subcellular localization by confocal microscopy
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: Constitutive promoter in S. nigrum stable transformation; 35S-based transient expression context in localization assays
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: High-value branch-switch engineering target for converting steroidal saponin-producing tissues into SGA-producing tissues or manipulating antinutritional SGA content; stacking with GAME4 increases flux redirection.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: SGAs are antinutritional and toxic, so increased activity may reduce food safety and increase bitterness; branch redirection can decrease steroidal saponin accumulation when combined with GAME4.
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: S. nigrum transcriptome RNA-seq, NCBI SRA: PRJNA1134384 (young leaves), PRJNA1133681 (green fruits)
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Evolved from canonical GABA-T via altered active-site specificity and loss of organellar targeting; spontaneous cyclization occurs after aminotransferase reaction rather than enzyme-catalyzed ring closure.

Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species: In vitro system; Nicotiana benthamiana; Black nightshade
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Late oxidation step before nitrogen incorporation
Pathway_Genes.Metabolic_Step_Position: late pathway; immediate upstream step to GAME12
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: forms immediate precursor for nitrogen incorporation and SGA commitment
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Substrate: Furostanol intermediate derived from protodioscin/uttroside B after deglycosylation
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Oxidizes furostanol at C-26 to the corresponding 26-furostanol aldehyde, which then serves as the amino acceptor for GAME12.
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key upstream control step supported but not formally designated rate-limiting
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: SGA-accumulating Solanum tissues; engineered S. nigrum leaves when coexpressed
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Plant defense; antinutritional toxicity and bitterness
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes SGA formation by producing the aldehyde precursor required for GAME12-mediated nitrogen incorporation; coexpression with GAME12 increases SGA levels and reduces uttroside B in S. nigrum leaves.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. GAME4 was biochemically characterized in this study as the long-proposed furostanol oxidase. 2. Reductive amination trapping indirectly confirmed that GAME4 converts furostanol to 26-furostanol aldehyde in vitro. 3. Coupled with GAME12, GAME4 enabled production of solasodine from protodioscin-derived substrate and soladulcidine from uttroside B-derived substrate. 4. In S. nigrum, low endogenous leaf expression of GAME4 likely limited flux into SGAs. 5. Co-overexpression of GAME4 with GAME12 caused ~4-fold higher SGA accumulation and decreased steroidal saponin uttroside B.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: In S. nigrum, expressed in green fruits and very low in young leaves relative to upstream GAME genes
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: In vitro assays requiring microsomes and NADPH; transient expression in N. benthamiana; constitutive overexpression in S. nigrum
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Microsomal enzyme assays with reductive amination trapping; coupled in vitro assays with GAME12; transient pathway reconstitution; stable co-overexpression in S. nigrum with metabolite profiling
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: Constitutive promoter in S. nigrum stable transformation
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful with GAME12 for redirecting steroidal metabolite flux toward SGAs or for pathway reconstruction/production of steroidal alkaloids in heterologous hosts.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Increasing GAME4 together with GAME12 reduces beneficial/non-nitrogenous steroidal saponin uttroside B and raises antinutritional SGAs.
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: S. nigrum transcriptome RNA-seq, NCBI SRA: PRJNA1134384 (young leaves), PRJNA1133681 (green fruits)
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: This paper provides the first biochemical validation of the previously hypothesized C-26 oxidase role.

Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: γ-aminobutyric acid transaminase (GABA-T) family; PLP-dependent aminotransferase
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Alternative aminotransferase branch for nitrogen incorporation into steroidal alkaloid aglycones
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: directly forms nitrogen-containing aglycone intermediate that commits flux toward SGAs
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalyzes transamination of the furostanol aldehyde precursor to introduce nitrogen into the steroid backbone, analogous to GAME12.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Potentially roots based on expression pattern
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Plant defense; antinutritional toxicity and bitterness
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Can promote SGA-forming aglycone production in heterologous and in vitro assays, indicating latent/parallel SGA-forming activity.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. A fourth tomato GABA-T homolog, GABA-T4, was identified. 2. It localizes to the cytosol and, unlike GABA-T1/GABA-T3, enabled dehydrotomatidine formation in N. benthamiana reconstitution assays. 3. It also efficiently produced solasodine in coupled in vitro assays. 4. GABA-T4 shows low canonical alanine-forming GABA shunt activity and is primarily expressed in roots. 5. Phylogenomics suggests it arose from Solanum-specific duplication and may have been lost in some lineages.
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Transient pathway reconstitution and in vitro coupled assays
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Confocal localization; transient pathway reconstitution in N. benthamiana; in vitro coupled enzyme assay with GAME4
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential alternative aminotransferase for SGA engineering, especially where tissue-specific expression or lineage-specific ortholog use is desired.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Could elevate toxic/antinutritional SGA production if deployed in edible tissues.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Not all Solanum species retain a GABA-T4 ortholog; functional role in native tomato SGA metabolism remains less established than GAME12.
